title: 'Meditate' senior-spring todo dump — Jan 14 2021
html:
TL;DR

  • Apple Note from Jan 14, 2021 (Yahir age 17, HS senior winter, ~five weeks after the Dec 12 Brenda/IDA todo dump). Six bullets: "-Meditate / -Submit U if O app / -advance on Forthan / -finish Activities chart / -submit u of o app / -take care of lola / -smile".
  • The duplicated "Submit U of O app" bullet (typed twice — once corrupted, once correct) marks the literal day Yahir submitted his University of Oregon application — significant because UO was the alternative path; he ended up at OSU instead. The note is the only timestamp surfaced for the UO leg of his application stack.
  • "-advance on Forthan" binds to Al Forthan Memorial Scholarship — Yahir is making progress on that essay the same day. Pairs with the May thank you for ford / al Forthan note for the full Forthan arc.
  • "-Meditate" + "-smile" as bracketing bullets is significant — Yahir is treating mood maintenance as a checklist item alongside scholarship logistics. Same instinct as the later therapy note from July 2021 ("-ask mom about a therapist"). Stress is being managed task-by-task. Read this against the Sept 2020 'Salvation' yogurt note for the same competence-as-self-soothing register.
  • "-take care of lola" — lola is the family dog, recurring through 2021's lists. Lola appears in * wash Lola* six months later — lifetime of being a small recurring obligation in Yahir's notes.

Key fragments

"-Meditate / -Submit U if O app / -advance on Forthan / -finish Activities chart / -submit u of o app / -take care of lola / -smile"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Only dated marker for the UO application submission — useful for the college-admissions sub-cluster.
  • Documents meditate-and-smile as todos — a structural signal that 17-year-old Yahir was running mood as a productivity input, predating the July 2021 therapy ask.
  • Densifies lola as a recurring care-obligation across multiple 2021 notes (so far: Jan 14, Jul 6).

Privacy

  • No PII — initials (UO, OSU) and entitied names (Forthan, Lola).

Wikilinks
